citations.narrative:
The facility has sections of the perimeter fence that do not meet the requirements:
-On the south side of the facility, part of the fencing adjacent to the main gate measures approximately 93 inches (7 feet
and 9 inches).
-On the south side of the facility, part of the fencing in front of a pond near the main gate measures approximately 92
inches (7 feet and 8 inches).
-On the south side of the facility, part of the fencing adjacent to the entrance to the water park measures approximately 92
inches (7 feet and 8 inches). During the inspection, the facility corrected this section by placing an additional high tension
wire at the top of the fence.
Failure to provide an appropriate perimeter fence may result in animal escape or allow persons or other animals to
interact with the regulated animals. All outdoor housing facilities must be enclosed by a perimeter fence that is of sufficient
height to keep animals and unauthorized person out. Fences less than 8 feet high (96 inches) for potentially dangerous
animals, such as but not limited to, large felines (e.g., lions, tigers, leopards, cougars, etc.), bears, wolves, rhinoceros,
and elephants, or less than 6 feet high for other animals must be approved in writing by the Administrator. The fence must
be constructed so that it protects the animals in the facility by restricting animals and unauthorized persons from going
through it or under it and having contact with the animals in the facility, and so that it can function as a secondary
containment system for the animals in the facility. It must be of sufficient distance from the outside of the primary
enclosure to prevent physical contact between animals inside the enclosure and animals or persons outside the perimeter
fence. Such fences less than 3 feet in distance from the primary enclosure must be approved in writing by the
Administrator. Correct by ensuring that all species requiring a perimeter fence are completely enclosed within an
adequate perimeter fence that meets all requirements. Alternatively, a variance may be requested in writing and sent to
the APHIS Administrator. Correct by 19-AUG-2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
n

narrative:
3.127(d) 
   
Facilities, outdoor. 
The facility has sections of the perimeter fence that do not meet the requirements:  
 
-On the south side of the facility, part of the fencing adjacent to the main gate measures approximately 93 inches (7 feet 
and 9 inches).  
-On the south side of the facility, part of the fencing in front of a pond near the main gate measures approximately 92 
inches (7 feet and 8 inches).  
-On the south side of the facility, part of the fencing adjacent to the entrance to the water park measures approximately 92 
inches (7 feet and 8 inches). During the inspection, the facility corrected this section by placing an additional high tension 
wire at the top of the fence. 
 
Failure to provide an appropriate perimeter fence may result in animal escape or allow persons or other animals to 
interact with the regulated animals. All outdoor housing facilities must be enclosed by a perimeter fence that is of sufficient 
height to keep animals and unauthorized person out. Fences less than 8 feet high (96 inches) for potentially dangerous 
animals, such as but not limited to, large felines (e.g., lions, tigers, leopards, cougars, etc.), bears, wolves, rhinoceros, 
and elephants, or less than 6 feet high for other animals must be approved in writing by the Administrator. The fence must 
be constructed so that it protects the animals in the facility by restricting animals and unauthorized persons from going 
through it or under it and having contact with the animals in the facility, and so that it can function as a secondary 
containment system for the animals in the facility. It must be of sufficient distance from the outside of the primary 
enclosure to prevent physical contact between animals inside the enclosure and animals or persons outside the perimeter 
fence. Such fences less than 3 feet in distance from the primary enclosure must be approved in writing by the 
Administrator. Correct by ensuring that all species requiring a perimeter fence are completely enclosed within an 
adequate perimeter fence that meets all requirements. Alternatively, a variance may be requested in writing and sent to 
the APHIS Administrator. Correct by 19-AUG-2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
